messages.content:
CVE-2022-22293 is a vulnerability in Dolibarr version 7.0.2 that allows HTML injection due to improper neutralization of user-controllable input. This vulnerability specifically affects the admin/limits.php component via the MAIN_MAX_DECIMALS_TOT parameter. It is categorized under CWE-79, which involves input that is not properly sanitized, enabling cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks.

Rated with a Medium severity, this flaw could be exploited by attackers to inject malicious scripts into web pages viewed by other users, potentially leading to unauthorized actions on behalf of the user or data theft. The vulnerability is outlined through MITRE ATT&CK Technique T1190, indicating its relevance to exploiting web application vulnerabilities.

Users of Dolibarr 7.0.2 are affected, and it is recommended to employ mitigation techniques including input validation and output encoding (M1048) to prevent such injections. Additional strategies include applying security patches, ensuring appropriate access controls (M1037), and conducting regular security audits (M1051) to minimize risk.
